predef.ADT.Relation.Binary._get_iterator.markdown: An iterator which makes all the left/right entities in the relation available as index/value pairs.

predef.ADT.Relation.Binary.contains.markdown:
```pike
mixed contains(mixed left, mixed right)
```

Return true/false: does the relation "left R right" exist?

predef.ADT.Relation.Binary.`().markdown:
```pike
mixed `()(mixed left, mixed right)
```

Does the same as the contains function: returns true if the relation "left R right" exists, and otherwise false.

predef.ADT.Relation.Binary.add.markdown:
```pike
object(this_program) add(mixed left, mixed right)
```

Adds "left R right" as a member of the relation. Returns the same relation.

predef.ADT.Relation.Binary.remove.markdown:
```pike
object(this_program) remove(mixed left, mixed right)
```

Removes "left R right" as a member of the relation. Returns the same relation.

predef.ADT.Relation.Binary.map.markdown:
```pike
array map(function f)
```

Maps every entry in the relation. The function f gets two arguments: the left and the right relation value. Returns an array with the return values of f for each and every mapped entry.
Note: since the entries in the relation are not ordered, the returned array will have its elements in no particular order. If you need to know which relation entry produced which result in the array, you have to make that information part of the value that f returns.

predef.ADT.Relation.Binary.filter.markdown:
```pike
object filter(function f)
```

Filters the entries in the relation, and returns a relation with all those entries for which the filtering function f returned true. The function f gets two arguments: the left and the right value for every entry in the relation.

predef.ADT.Relation.Binary.filter_destru.markdown:
```pike
object(this_program) filter_destructively(function f)
```

Filters the entries in the relation destructively, removing all entries for which the filtering function f returns false. The function f gets two arguments: the left and the right value for each entry in the relation.

predef.ADT.Relation.Binary._sizeof.markdown:
```pike
mixed _sizeof()
```

Returns the number of relation entries in the relation. (Or with other words: the number of relations in the relation set.)

predef.ADT.Relation.Binary.`&.markdown:
```pike
mixed `&(mixed rel)
```

The expression `rel1 & rel2' returns a new relation which has those and only those relation entries that are present in both rel1 and rel2.

predef.ADT.Relation.Binary.`-.markdown:
```pike
mixed `-(mixed rel)
```

The expression `rel1 - rel2' returns a new relation which has those and only those relation entries that are present in rel1 and not present in rel2.

predef.ADT.Relation.Binary.make_symmetri.markdown:
```pike
object(this_program) make_symmetric()
```

Makes the relation symmetric, i.e. makes sure that if xRy is part of the relation set, then yRx should also be a part of the relation set.

predef.ADT.Relation.Binary.find_shortest.signature: array find_shortest_path(mixed from, mixed to, void|multiset avoiding)
predef.ADT.Relation.Binary.find_shortest.markdown:
```pike
array find_shortest_path(mixed from, mixed to, void|multiset avoiding)
```

Assuming the relation's domain and range sets are equal, and that the relation xRy means "there is a path from node x to node y", find_shortest_path attempts to find a path with a minimum number of steps from one given node to another. The path is returned as an array of nodes (including the starting and ending node), or 0 if no path was found. If several equally short paths exist, one of them will be chosen pseudorandomly.
Trying to find a path from a node to itself will always succeed, returning an array of one element: the node itself. (Or in other words, a path with no steps, only a starting/ending point).
The argument avoiding is either 0 (or omitted), or a multiset of nodes that must not be part of the path.

predef.ADT.Heap.push.markdown:
```pike
object(Element) push(mixed value)
```

Push an element onto the heap. The heap will automatically sort itself so that the smallest value will be at the top.
**Returns:** Returns an element handle, which can be used with adjust() and remove().
**Note:** If value is a Heap.Element and already present on the heap this is equivalent to calling adjust().
**See also:** pop(), remove()

predef.ADT.Heap.adjust.markdown:
```pike
object(Element) adjust(mixed value)
```

Takes a value in the heap and sorts it through the heap to maintain its sort criteria (increasing order).
- `value` — Either the element handle returned by push(), or the pushed value itself.
**Returns:** Returns the element handle for the value (if present in the heap), and `0` (zero) otherwise.

predef.ADT.Heap.low_pop.markdown:
```pike
object(Element) low_pop()
```

Removes and returns the Element on top of the heap, which also is the smallest value in the heap.
**Returns:** Returns UNDEFINED if the heap is empty.
**See also:** pop(), peek(), push(), remove()

predef.ADT.Heap.pop.markdown:
```pike
mixed pop()
```

Removes and returns the item on top of the heap, which also is the smallest value in the heap.
**Throws:** Throws an error if the heap is empty.
**See also:** low_pop(), peek(), push(), remove()

predef.ADT.Heap._sizeof.markdown:
```pike
int _sizeof()
```

Returns the number of elements in the heap.

predef.ADT.Heap.top.markdown:
```pike
"deprecated"mixed top()
```

Removes and returns the item on top of the heap, which also is the smallest value in the heap.
**Deprecated:** No longer recommended.

predef.ADT.Heap.size.markdown:
```pike
"deprecated"int size()
```

Returns the number of elements in the heap.
**Deprecated:** No longer recommended.

predef.ADT.Heap.low_peek.markdown:
```pike
object(Element) low_peek()
```

Returns the Element on top of the heap (which is also the one with the smallest value in the heap) without removing it.
**Returns:** Returns the smallest Element on the heap if any, and `UNDEFINED` otherwise.
**See also:** peek(), low_pop(), pop()

predef.ADT.Heap.peek.markdown:
```pike
mixed peek()
```

Returns the item on top of the heap (which is also the smallest value in the heap) without removing it.
**Returns:** Returns the smallest value on the heap if any, and `UNDEFINED` otherwise.
**See also:** low_peek(), pop()

predef.ADT.Heap.remove.markdown:
```pike
void remove(mixed value)
```

Remove a value from the heap.
- `value` — Value to remove.
**See also:** push(), pop()

Push an element val into the priority queue and assign a priority value pri to it. The priority queue will automatically sort itself so that the element with the smallest priority will be at the top.

predef.ADT.Priority_queue.peek.markdown:




This method returns the contents of a given row as an array.

predef.ADT.Table.table.`==.markdown:


This method compares two tables. They are equal if the contents of the tables and the column names are equal. The column name comparison is case insensitive.

predef.ADT.Table.table.append_bottom.markdown:
```pike
object append_bottom(object table)
```

This method appends two tables. The table given as an argument will be added at the bottom of the current table. Note, the column names must be equal. The column name comparison is case insensitive.

predef.ADT.Table.table.append_right.markdown:
```pike

```

This method appends two tables. The table given as an argument will be added on the right side of the current table. Note that the number of rows in both tables must be equal.

predef.ADT.Table.table.select.markdown:
```pike
object select(int|string ... columns)
```

This method returns a new table object with the selected columns only.

predef.ADT.Table.table.remove.markdown:
```pike
object remove(int|string ... columns)
```

Like select(), but the given columns will not be in the resulting table.

predef.ADT.Table.table.where.signature: object where(array(int|string)|int|string columns, function f, mixed ... args)
predef.ADT.Table.table.where.markdown:
```pike
object where(array(int|string)|int|string columns, function f, mixed ... args)
```

This method calls the function for each row. If the function returns zero, the row will be thrown away. If the function returns something non-zero, the row will be kept. The result will be returned as a new table object.

predef.ADT.Table.table.group.signature: object(this_program) group(mapping(int|string : function)|function f, mixed ... args)
predef.ADT.Table.table.group.markdown:
```pike
object(this_program) group(mapping(int|string : function)|function f, mixed ... args)
```

This method calls the function f for each column each time a non uniqe row will be joined. The table will be grouped by the columns not listed. The result will be returned as a new table object.

predef.ADT.Table.table.sum.markdown:
```pike
object(this_program) sum(int|string ... columns)
```

This method sums all equal rows. The table will be grouped by the columns not listed. The result will be returned as a new table object.

predef.ADT.Table.table.distinct.markdown:
```pike
object(this_program) distinct(int|string ... columns)
```

This method groups by the given columns and returns a table with only unique rows. When no columns are given, all rows will be unique. A new table object will be returned.

predef.ADT.Table.table.map.signature: object map(function f, array(int|string)|int|string columns, mixed ... args)
predef.ADT.Table.table.map.markdown:
```pike
object map(function f, array(int|string)|int|string columns, mixed ... args)
```

This method calls the function f for all rows in the table. The value returned will replace the values in the columns given as argument to map. If the function returns an array, several columns will be replaced. Otherwise the first column will be replaced. The result will be returned as a new table object.

predef.ADT.Table.table.sort.markdown:
```pike
object sort(int|string ... columns)
```

This method sorts the table in ascendent order on one or several columns and returns a new table object. The left most column is sorted last. Note that the sort is stable.
**See also:** rsort()

predef.ADT.Table.table.rsort.markdown:
```pike
object rsort(int|string ... columns)
```

Like sort(), but in descending order.

predef.ADT.Table.table.limit.markdown:

This method truncates the table to the first n rows and returns a new object.

predef.ADT.Table.table.rename.markdown:


This method renames the column named from to to and returns a new table object. Note that from can be the column position.

predef.ADT.Table.table.type.markdown:
```pike
mapping type(int|string column, void|mapping type)
```

This method gives the type for the given column.
If a second argument is given, the old type will be replaced with type. The column type is only used when the table is displayed. The format is as specified in create().

predef.ADT.Table.table.create.signature: void create(array(array) table, array(string) column_names, array(mapping(string : string))|void column_types)
predef.ADT.Table.table.create.markdown:
```pike
void create(array(array) table, array(string) column_names, array(mapping(string : string))|void column_types)
```

The ADT.Table.table class takes two or three arguments:
- `table` — The first argument is a two-dimensional array consisting of one array of columns per row. All rows must have the same number of columns as specified in column_names.
- `column_names` — This argument is an array of column names associated with each column in the table. References by column name are case insensitive. The case used in column_names will be used when the table is displayed. A column can also be referred to by its position, starting from zero.
- `column_types` — This is an optional array of mappings. The column type information is only used when displaying the table. Currently, only the keyword `"type"` is recognized. The type can be specified as `"text"` or `"num"` (numerical). Text columns are left adjusted, whereas numerical columns are right adjusted. If a mapping in the array is 0 (zero), it will be assumed to be a text column. If column_types is omitted, all columns will displayed as text. See ADT.Table.ASCII.encode() on how to display a table.
**See also:** ADT.Table.ASCII.encode()


Returns 1 if the set is empty, otherwise 0.

predef.ADT.Set.map.markdown:
```pike
array map(function f)
```

Map the values of a set: calls the map function f once for each member of the set, returning an array which contains the result of each one of those function calls. Note that since a set isn't ordered, the values in the returned array will be in more or less random order. If you need to know which member value produced which result, you have to make that a part of what the filtering function returns.
The filtering function f is called with a single, mixed-type argument which is the member value to be mapped.

predef.ADT.Set.filter.markdown:
```pike
object(this_program) filter(function f)
```

Return a filtered version of the set, containing only those members for which the filtering function f returned true.
The filtering function is called with a single mixed-type argument which is the member value to be checked.

predef.ADT.Set.filter_destructively.markdown:
```pike
object(this_program) filter_destructively(function f)
```

Destructively filter the set, i.e. remove every element for which the filtering function f returns 0, and then return the set.
The filtering function is called with a single mixed-type argument which is the member value to be checked.

predef.ADT.Set.subset.markdown:


Subset. A <= B returns true if all items in A are also present in B.

predef.ADT.Set.superset.markdown:
```pike

```

Superset. A >= B returns true if all items in B are also present in A.

predef.ADT.Set.`==.markdown:
```pike
int(0..1) `==(object(ADT.Set) other)
```

Equality. A == B returns true if all items in A are present in B, and all items in B are present in A. Otherwise, it returns false.

predef.ADT.Set.`<.markdown:
```pike
int(0..1) `<(object(ADT.Set) other)
```

True subset. A < B returns true if each item in A is also present in B, and B contains at least one item not present in A.

predef.ADT.Set.`>.markdown:
```pike
int(0..1) `>(object(ADT.Set) other)
```

True superset. A > B returns true if each item in B is also present in A, and A contains at least one item not present in B.

predef.ADT.Set.`|.markdown:
```pike
object(this_program) `|(object(ADT.Set) other)
```

Union. Returns a set containing all elements present in either or both of the operand sets.

predef.ADT.Set.`&.markdown:
```pike
object(this_program) `&(object(ADT.Set) other)
```

Intersection. Returns a set containing those values that were present in both the operand sets.

predef.ADT.Set.`-.markdown:
```pike
object(this_program) `-(object(ADT.Set) other)
```

Difference. The expression 'A - B', where A and B are sets, returns all elements in A that are not also present in B.

predef.ADT.Set.`[].markdown:


Indexing a set with a value V gives 1 if V is a member of the set, otherwise 0.

predef.ADT.Set.`[]=.markdown:
```pike

```

Setting an index V to 0 removes V from the set. Setting it to a non-0 value adds V as a member of the set.

predef.ADT.Set._indices.markdown:
```pike
array _indices()
```

In analogy with multisets, indices() of an ADT.Set givess an array containing all members of the set.

predef.ADT.Set._values.markdown:
```pike
array _values()
```

In analogy with multisets, values() of an ADT.Set givess an array indicating the number of occurrences in the set for each position in the member array returned by indices(). (Most of the time, this is probably rather useless for sets, since the result is an array which just contain 1's, one for each member of the set. Still, this function is provided for consistency.

```pike
void remove_uri(string|object(Standards.URI) uri)
```

Remove URI from the database.

predef.Search.Database.Base.remove_uri_p.markdown:

```pike
void remove_uri_prefix(string|object(Standards.URI) uri)
```

Remove URI prefix from the database.
- `uri` — The URI prefix of the resource being removed.

predef.Search.Database.Base.remove_docum.markdown:
```pike
void remove_document(string|object(Standards.URI) uri, void|string language)
```

Remove a document from the database.
- `uri` — The URI of the resource being indexed.
- `language` — A two letter ISO-639-1 language code. If zero, delete all existing language forks with the URI of uri.

```pike
void remove_document_prefix(string|object(Standards.URI) uri)
```

Removes all documents that matches the provided uri prefix.
- `uri` — The URI prefix of the documents to delete.

predef.Search.Database.Base.sync.markdown:
```pike
void sync()
```

Writes the data stored in temporary buffers to permanent storage. Calls the function set by set_sync_callback] when done.

predef.Search.Database.Base.set_sync_cal.markdown:
```pike
void set_sync_callback(function f)
```

Sets a function to be called when sync has been completed.

predef.Search.Database.Base.get_blob.signature: string get_blob(string word, int num, void|mapping(string : mapping(int : string)) blobcache)
predef.Search.Database.Base.get_blob.markdown:
```pike
string get_blob(string word, int num, void|mapping(string : mapping(int : string)) blobcache)
```

Retrieves a blob from the database.
- `word` — The wanted word. Possibly in wide-string format. (Not UTF-8 encoded.)
- `num` — (no description)
**Returns:** The blob requested, or 0 if there's no more blobs.

predef.Search.Database.Base.allocate_fie.markdown:
```pike
int allocate_field_id(string field)
```

Allocate a field id.
- `field` — The (possibly wide string) field name wanted.
**Returns:** An allocated numeric id, or -1 if the allocation failed.

predef.Search.Database.Base.get_field_id.markdown:
```pike
int get_field_id(string field, void|int do_not_create)
```

Retrieve and possibly creates the numeric id of a field
- `field` — The (possibly wide string) field name wanted.
- `do_not_create` — If non-zero, do not allocate a field id for this field
**Returns:** An allocated numeric id, or -1 if it did not exist, or allocation failed.

predef.Search.Database.Base.remove_field.markdown:
```pike
void remove_field(string field)
```

Remove a field from the database. Also removes all stored metadata with this field, but not all indexed words using this field id.
- `field` — The (possibly wide string) field name to be removed.

predef.Search.Database.Base.safe_remove_.markdown:
```pike
void safe_remove_field(string field)
```

Remove a field from the database if it isn't used by the filters. Also removes all stored metadata with this field, but not all indexed words using this field id.
- `field` — The (possibly wide string) field name to be removed.

predef.Search.Database.Base.list_fields.markdown:
```pike
mapping(string : int) list_fields()
```

Lists all fields in the search database.
**Returns:** A mapping with the fields in the index part, and the corresponding numeric field id as values.

predef.Search.Database.Base.get_language.markdown:
```pike
mapping(string|int : int) get_language_stats()
```

Retrieve statistics about the number of documents in different languages.
**Returns:** A mapping with the the language code in the index part, and the corresponding number of documents as values.

predef.Search.Database.Base.get_num_word.markdown:
```pike
int get_num_words()
```

Returns the number of distinct words in the database.

predef.Search.Database.Base.get_database.markdown:
```pike
int get_database_size()
```

Returns the size, in bytes, of the search database.

predef.Search.Database.Base.get_num_dele.markdown:
```pike
int get_num_deleted_documents()
```

Returns the number of deleted documents in the database.

predef.Search.Database.Base.get_most_com.markdown:
```pike
array(array) get_most_common_words(void|int count)
```

Returns a list of the count most common words in the database. count defaults to `10`.

predef.Search.Database.Base.list_url_by_.signature: void list_url_by_prefix(string url_prefix, function(string : void) cb)
predef.Search.Database.Base.list_url_by_.markdown:
```pike
void list_url_by_prefix(string url_prefix, function(string : void) cb)
```

Calls cb for all uri:s that match uri_prefix.

predef.Search.RankingProfile.create.signature: void create(void|int cutoff, void|array(int) proximity_ranking, void|object(Search.Database.Base) db, void|array(int)|mapping(string : int) field_ranking)
predef.Search.RankingProfile.create.markdown:
```pike
void create(void|int cutoff, void|array(int) proximity_ranking, void|object(Search.Database.Base) db, void|array(int)|mapping(string : int) field_ranking)
```

cutoff defaults to 8, proximity_ranking defaults to `({ 8, 7, 6, 5, 4, 3, 2, 1, })` and field_ranking defaults to `({ 17, 0, 147 }) + allocate(62)`. db is only needed if field_ranking is provided as a mapping.

predef.Search.RankingProfile.copy.markdown:

```pike
object(this_program) copy()
```

Returns a copy of this object.

predef.Search.Queue.MySQL.create.signature: void create(object(Web.Crawler.Stats) _stats, object(Web.Crawler.Policy) _policy, string _url, string _table, void|object(Web.Crawler.RuleSet) _allow, void|object(Web.Crawler.RuleSet) _deny)

```pike
void create(object(Web.Crawler.Stats) _stats, object(Web.Crawler.Policy) _policy, string _url, string _table, void|object(Web.Crawler.RuleSet) _allow, void|object(Web.Crawler.RuleSet) _deny)
```

- `_url` — Sql.Sql URL for the database to store the queue.
- `_table` — Sql.Sql table name to store the queue in. If the table doesn't exist it will be created.

predef.Search.Queue.MySQL.get_schemes.markdown:

```pike
array(string) get_schemes()
```

**Returns:** Returns an array with all URI schemes currently used in the queue.

```pike
int get_stage(object(Standards.URI) uri)
```

**Returns:** Returns the current stage for the specified URI.
**See also:** set_stage()

predef.Search.Queue.MySQL.reset_stage.markdown:
```pike
void reset_stage(string|void uri_prefix)
```

Reset the stage to `0` (zero) for all URIs with the specified uri_prefix. If no uri_prefix is specified reset the stage for all URIs.

predef.Search.Queue.Base.add_uri.signature: void add_uri(object(Standards.URI) uri, int recurse, string template, void|int force)
predef.Search.Queue.Base.add_uri.markdown:
```pike
void add_uri(object(Standards.URI) uri, int recurse, string template, void|int force)
```

Add an URI to be crawled.

predef.Search.Queue.Base.set_md5.markdown:
```pike
void set_md5(object(Standards.URI) uri, string md5)
```

Set the content MD5 for an URI.

predef.Search.Queue.Base.set_recurse.markdown:
```pike
void set_recurse(object(Standards.URI) uri, int recurse)
```

Set the recurse mode for an URI.

predef.Search.Queue.Base.get_extra.markdown:
```pike
mapping get_extra(object(Standards.URI) uri)
```

**Returns:** Returns a mapping with the current state for the URI.
Currently this function always returns a `mapping(string:string)`, but this may change to the above in the future.

predef.Search.Queue.Base.get_uris.markdown:
```pike
array(object(Standards.URI)) get_uris(void|int stage)
```

**Returns:** Returns all URIs if no stage is specified, otherwise returns the URIs at the specified stage.
State `0` (zero) is a special case, and returns all URIs. This may change in the future.

predef.Search.Queue.Base.put.markdown:
```pike
void put(string|array(string)|object(Standards.URI)|array(object(Standards.URI)) uri)
```

Add one or multiple URIs to the queue.
All the URIs will be added with recursion enabled and an empty template.

predef.Search.Queue.Base.clear.markdown:
```pike
void clear()
```

Clear and empty the entire queue.

predef.Search.Queue.Base.clear_stage.markdown:

```pike
void clear_stage(int ... stages)
```

Reset all URIs at the specified stage to stage `0` (zero).

predef.Search.Queue.Base.remove_stage.markdown:
```pike
void remove_stage(int stage)
```

Remove all URIs at the specified stage.

predef.Search.Queue.Base.clear_md5.markdown:

```pike
void clear_md5(int ... stages)
```

Clear the content MD5 for all URIs at the specified stages.

predef.Search.Queue.Base.num_with_stage.markdown:
```pike
int num_with_stage(int ... stage)
```

**Returns:** Returns the number of URIs at the specified stage(s).

predef.Search.Queue.Base.set_stage.markdown:
```pike
void set_stage(object(Standards.URI) uri, int stage)
```

Set the stage for a single URI.

predef.Search.Queue.Base.remove_uri.markdown:

```pike
void remove_uri(string|object(Standards.URI) uri)
```

Remove an URI from the queue.

predef.Search.Queue.Base.remove_uri_pref.markdown:
```pike
void remove_uri_prefix(string|object(Standards.URI) uri)
```

Remove all URIs with the specified prefix from the queue.

predef.Search.Queue.Base.clear_cache.markdown:

```pike
void clear_cache()
```

Clear any RAM caches.

predef.Search.Query.execute.signature: array(object(Search.ResultSet)|array(string)) execute(object(Search.Database.Base) db, object(Search.Grammar.AbstractParser) parser, string query, object(Search.RankingProfile) ranking, void|array(string) stop_words, void|object(search_order) order)


- `query` — The query string entered by user.
- `db` — The search database.
- `defaultRanking` — Used when searching in the field "any:".

```pike
constant contenttypes
```

The MIME content types this class can filter.


predef.Search.Filter.Output.fields.markdown:

```pike
mapping(string : string) fields
```

Data extracted from input, grouped by type. Standard fields are `"body"`, `"title"`, `"description"`, `"keywords"` and `"mtime"`.
**Note:** Note that all field values (even `"mtime"`) are strings.

predef.Search.Filter.Output.document_siz.markdown:

```pike
int document_size
```

The size of the document.

```pike
mapping(string : string) uri_anchors
```

Maps un-normalized URLs to raw text, e.g. ` ([ "http://pike.lysator.liu.se": "Pike language" ]) `.

predef.Search.Filter.Output.links.markdown:
```pike
array(object(Standards.URI)|string) links
```

All links collected from the document.

predef.Search.Filter.Output.fix_relative.markdown:

```pike
void fix_relative_links(object(Standards.URI) base_uri)
```

Modifies relative links in links to be relative to base_uri.

predef.Tools.PV.PVImage.markdown:
```pike
typedef PVImage
```

The image types accepted. If the image is a string, it is assumed to be a filename of a image that can be loaded with Image.load. This includes URLs.

predef.Tools.PV.set_alpha_mode.markdown:
```pike
void set_alpha_mode(object(AlphaMode) m)
```

Set the alpha combining mode. m is one of Squares, Solid, None and AlphaOnly.

predef.Tools.PV.set_alpha_colors.markdown:
```pike
void set_alpha_colors(object(Image.Color.Color) c1, object(Image.Color.Color)|void c2)
```

Set the colors used for the alpha combination. c2 is only used for the Squares alpha mode.
**See also:** set_alpha_mode()

predef.Tools.PV.get_as_image.markdown:
```pike
object(Image.Image) get_as_image(object(PVImage) i)
```

Return the current image as a Image object, with the alpha combining done.

predef.Tools.PV.set_image.markdown:
```pike
void set_image(object(PVImage) i)
```

Change the image.

predef.Tools.PV.scale.markdown:
```pike
void scale(float factor)
```

Scale the image before display with the specified factor.

predef.Tools.PV.save.markdown:
```pike
void save(string filename, string|void format)
```

Write the image to a file. If no format is specified, PNG is used. The alpha combination is done on the image before it's saved.

predef.Tools.Install.ProgressBar.markdown: A class keeping some methods and state to conveniently render ASCII progress bars to stdout.

predef.Tools.Install.ProgressBar.set_cur.markdown:
```pike
void set_current(int _cur)
```

Change the amount of progress without updating on stdout.

predef.Tools.Install.ProgressBar.set_nam.markdown:
```pike
void set_name(string _name)
```

Change the name of the progress bar without updating on stdout.

predef.Tools.Install.ProgressBar.update.markdown:
```pike
int update(int increment)
```

Write the current look of the progressbar to stdout.
- `increment` — the number of increments closer to completion since last call
**Returns:** the length (in characters) of the line with the progressbar

predef.Tools.Install.ProgressBar.create.signature: void create(string name, int cur, int max, float|void phase_base, float|void phase_size)
predef.Tools.Install.ProgressBar.create.markdown:
```pike
void create(string name, int cur, int max, float|void phase_base, float|void phase_size)
```

- `name` — The name (printed in the first 13 columns of the row)
- `cur` — How much progress has been made so far
- `max` — The amount of progress signifying 100% done. Must be greater than zero.

predef.Tools.Install.features.markdown:
```pike
array(string) features()
```

Return an array of enabled features.
**Note:** Used by the master when given the option `--features`.
**See also:** Tools.Standalone.features

predef.Tools.sed.`().signature: string|array `()(string|array(string) commands, string|array(string) data, void|int suppress)
predef.Tools.sed.`().markdown:

```pike
string|array `()(string|array(string) commands, string|array(string) data, void|int suppress)
```

predef.Tools.Legal.License.get_text.markdown:
```pike
string get_text()
```

Returns all the licenses as a string, suitable for saving as a file.

predef.Tools.Legal.Copyright.add.markdown:
```pike
void add(string what, array(string) holders)
```

Adds a copyright message for the copyright holders for the component what.
**Throws:** An error is thrown if the copyrighted component what is already in the list of copyrights.

predef.Tools.Legal.Copyright.get_latest_.markdown:
```pike
string get_latest_pike()
```

Return the latest copyright holder of Pike.

predef.Tools.Legal.Copyright.get_all.markdown:
```pike
mapping(string : array(string)) get_all()
```

Returns a mapping containing all the stored copyrights. The mapping maps component name to an array of copyright holders.

predef.Tools.Legal.Copyright.get_text.markdown:
```pike
string get_text()
```

Returns the copyrights as a string, suitable for saving as a file.

predef.Tools.Shoot.Test.name.markdown:
```pike
constant name
```

The name of the test.

predef.Tools.Shoot.Test.perform.markdown:
```pike
int perform(mixed|void context)
```

perform() is the function called in the tests, when it returns the test is complete.
The function returns the number of whatever the test did.

predef.Tools.Shoot.Test.prepare.markdown:
```pike
mixed prepare()
```

If this function exists, it computes the context to pass to the perform() function. The time consumed by this function will not count towards the test.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Command.help.markdown:
```pike
string help(string what)
```

Returns a one line description of the command. This help should be shorter than 54 characters.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Command.doc.markdown:
```pike
string doc(string what, string with)
```

A more elaborate documentation of the command. This should be less than 68 characters per line.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Command.exec.signature: void exec(object(Evaluator) e, string line, array(string) words, array(string) tokens)
predef.Tools.Hilfe.Command.exec.markdown:
```pike
void exec(object(Evaluator) e, string line, array(string) words, array(string) tokens)
```

The actual command callback. Messages to the user should be written out by using the safe_write method in the Evaluator object.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.CommandReset.markdown: Variable reset command. Put ___Hilfe->commands->reset = Tools.Hilfe.CommandReset(); in your .hilferc to have this command defined when you open Hilfe.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Expression.create.markdown:
```pike
void create(array(string) t)
```

- `t` — An array of Pike tokens.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Expression._sizeof.markdown:
```pike
int _sizeof()
```

The number of non-whitespace tokens in the expression.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Expression.`[].markdown:
```pike
string `[](int f, void|int t)
```

Returns a token or a token range without whitespaces.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Expression.`[]=.markdown:
```pike
string `[]=(int f, string v)
```

Replaces a token with a new token.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Expression.depth.markdown:
```pike
int depth(int f)
```

Return the parenthesis depth at the given position.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Expression.in_sscanf.markdown:
```pike
int(0..1) in_sscanf(int f)
```

Returns 1 if the current position is within a sscanf expression.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Expression.check_modi.markdown:
```pike
string check_modifiers()
```

See if there are any forbidden modifiers used in the expression, e.g. "private int x;" is not valid inside Hilfe.
**Returns:** Returns an error message as a string if the expression contains a forbidden modifier, otherwise `0`.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Expression.code.markdown:
```pike
string code()
```

Returns the expression verbatim.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Expression.endoftype.markdown:
```pike
int endoftype(int position)
```

Returns at which position the type declaration that begins at position position ends. A return value of -1 means that the token or tokens from position can not be a type declaration.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Expression.find_match.markdown:
```pike
int find_matching(string token, int|void pos)
```

Returns the position of the matching parenthesis of the given kind, starting from the given position. The position should be the position after the opening parenthesis, or later. Assuming balanced code. Returns -1 on failure.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Expression.is_block.markdown:
```pike
int(0..1) is_block(int pos)
```

Is there a block starting at pos?

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Expression.cast.markdown:
```pike
mixed cast(string to)
```

An Expression object can be cast to an array or a string. In both forms all tokens, including white spaces will be returned.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.ParserState.markdown: In every Hilfe object (Evaluator) there is a ParserState object that manages the current state of the parser. Essentially tokens are entered in one end and complete expressions are output in the other. The parser object is accessible as ___Hilfe->state from Hilfe expressions.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.ParserState.feed.markdown:
```pike
void feed(array(string) tokens)
```

Feed more tokens into the state.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.ParserState.read.markdown:
```pike
array(object(Expression)) read()
```

Read out completed expressions. Returns an array where every element is an expression represented as an array of tokens.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.ParserState.show_erro.markdown:
```pike
void show_error(function(array(string)|string, mixed ... : int) w)
```

Prints out any error that might have occurred while push_string was executed. The error will be printed with the print function w.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.ParserState.push_stri.markdown:
```pike
array(string) push_string(string line)
```

Sends the input line to Parser.Pike for tokenization, but keeps a state between each call to handle multiline /**/ comments and multiline #"" strings.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.ParserState.datap.markdown:
```pike
int datap()
```

Returns true if there is any waiting expression that can be fetched with read.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.ParserState.finishedp.markdown:
```pike
int(0..1) finishedp()
```

Are we in the middle of an expression. Used e.g. for changing the Hilfe prompt when entering multiline expressions.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.ParserState.flush.markdown:
```pike
void flush()
```

Clear the current state.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.ParserState.status.markdown:
```pike
string status()
```

Returns the current parser state. Used by "dump state".

predef.Tools.Hilfe.HilfeHistory.markdown: In every Hilfe object (Evaluator) there is a HilfeHistory object that manages the result history. That history object is accessible both from __ and ___Hilfe->history in Hilfe expressions.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Evaluator.commands.markdown:
```pike
mapping(string : object(Command)) commands
```

This mapping contains the available Hilfe commands, including the built in ones (dump, exit, help, new, quit), so it is possible to replace or remove them. The name of a command should be 10 characters or less.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Evaluator.state.markdown:
```pike
object(ParserState) state
```

Keeps the state, e.g. multiline input in process etc.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Evaluator.variables.markdown:
```pike
mapping(string : mixed) variables
```

The locally defined variables (name:value).

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Evaluator.types.markdown:
```pike
mapping(string : string) types
```

The types of the locally defined variables (name:type).

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Evaluator.constants.markdown:
```pike
mapping(string : mixed) constants
```

The locally defined constants (name:value).

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Evaluator.functions.markdown:
```pike
mapping(string : function) functions
```

The locally defined functions (name:value).

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Evaluator.programs.markdown:
```pike
mapping(string : program) programs
```

The locally defined programs (name:value).

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Evaluator.history.markdown:
```pike
object(HilfeHistory) history
```

The current result history.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Evaluator.write.markdown:
```pike
array|object|function(string : int) write
```

The function to use when writing to the user.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Evaluator.add_writer.markdown:
```pike
void add_writer(object|function(string : int) new)
```

Adds another output function.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Evaluator.remove_writ.markdown:
```pike
void remove_writer(object|function old)
```

Removes an output function.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Evaluator.safe_write.markdown:
```pike
int safe_write(array(string)|string in, mixed ... args)
```

An output method that shouldn't crash.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Evaluator.add_input_h.markdown:
```pike
void add_input_hook(function|object new)
```

Adds a function to the input hook, making all user data be fed into the function.
**See also:** remove_input_hook

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Evaluator.remove_inpu.markdown:
```pike
void remove_input_hook(function|object old)
```

Removes a function from the input hook.
**See also:** add_input_hook

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Evaluator.print_versi.markdown:
```pike
void print_version()
```

Displays the current version of Hilfe.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Evaluator.reset_evalu.markdown:
```pike
void reset_evaluator()
```

Clears the current state, history and removes all locally defined variables, constants, functions and programs. Removes all imports and inherits. It does not reset the command mapping nor reevaluate the .hilferc file.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Evaluator.add_input_l.markdown:
```pike
void add_input_line(string s)
```

Input a line of text into Hilfe. It checks if s is ".", in which case it calls state->flush(). Otherwise just calls add_buffer.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Evaluator.add_buffer.markdown:
```pike
void add_buffer(string s)
```

Add buffer tokenizes the input string and determines if the new line is a Hilfe command. If not, it updates the current state with the new tokens and sends any and all complete expressions to evaluation in parse_expression.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Evaluator.parse_expre.markdown:
```pike
string parse_expression(object(Expression) expr)
```

Parses a Pike expression. Returns 0 if everything went well, or a string with an error message otherwise.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Evaluator.last_compil.markdown:
```pike
string last_compiled_expr
```

The last created wrapper in which an expression was evaluated.

```pike
int last_compile_time
```

The last compile time;

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Evaluator.last_eval_t.markdown:

```pike
int last_eval_time
```

The last evaluation time;

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Evaluator.trace_level.markdown:
```pike
int trace_level
```

The current trace level.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Evaluator.assembler_d.markdown:

```pike
int assembler_debug_level
```

The current assembler debug level. Only available if Pike is compiled with RTL debug.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Evaluator.compiler_tr.markdown:

```pike
int compiler_trace_level
```

The current compiler trace level. Only available if Pike is compiled with RTL debug.

predef.Tools.Hilfe.Evaluator.debug_level.markdown:

```pike
int debug_level
```

The current debug level. Only available if Pike is compiled with RTL debug.

predef.Tools.Standalone.forkd.ForkStream.markdown:
This class maps 1 to 1 to Process.RemoteProcess, and implements the daemon side of the RPC protocol.
It contains an array (fds) with the file descriptors that have been received so far from the remote.

```pike
array(object(Stdio.Fd)) fds
```

The remote file descriptors received so far in order.

predef.Tools.Standalone.forkd.FdStream.markdown:
This is the main control Stdio.Fd and is always on fd number `3`.
To spawn a new process, a new Stdio.PROP_SEND_FD capable Stdio.Fd is sent over this fd, and a single byte of data is sent as payload.
The sent fd will become a ForkFd inside a ForkStream.

predef.Tools.Standalone.process_files.ve.markdown:
```pike
string version
```

Your hack's version number. If you version control your file with cvs, we suggest you set the contents of this variable to something that that will automatically expand to a number for every new revision, for instance
**Example:** string version = sprintf("%d.%d.%d",(int)__REAL_VERSION__,__REAL_MINOR__,__REAL_BUILD__);

predef.Tools.Standalone.process_files.de.markdown:
```pike

```

One-liner that gets shown for this tool when running `pike -x` without additional options. (Assuming your tool resides in `Standalone.pmod`.) Does not include the name of the tool itself; just provide a nice, terse description, ending with a period for conformity.

predef.Tools.Standalone.process_files.us.markdown:
```pike
string usage
```

Long description of the purpose and usage of your tool, for --help and the case where not enough options are given on the command line. Its invocation mode (for instance "pike -x yourtool", when invoked that way) is prepended, and a list of available flags appended to this description.

predef.Tools.Standalone.process_files.wa.markdown:


The number of (mandatory) command-line options your hack needs and which your process callback wants (beside the input file). By default 0.

```pike

```

Gets called with the contents of a file (or stdin). Return your output, or 0 if you didn't do anything. args are the first want_args command-line options provided, before the list of files to process.

predef.Tools.Standalone.process_files.ve.markdown:


0 in quiet mode, 1 by default, above = more output

predef.Tools.Standalone.process_files.ov.markdown:


0 to make backups, 1 to overwrite (default)

predef.Tools.Standalone.process_files.re.markdown:


1 to recurse into directories, 0 not to (default)

predef.Tools.Standalone.process_files.de.markdown:


Takes the path to a file and the first want_args command-line options provided, before the list of files to process. You might want to override this method if your tool needs to see file paths.
**See also:** process_path

```pike
int process_path(string path, string ... args)
```

Takes the path to a file or directory and the first want_args first command-line options provided, before the list of files to process. You might want to override this method if your tool needs to see all paths.
**See also:** process_file


- `issuer` — Distinguished name for the issuer.
- `rsa` — RSA parameters for the issuer.

predef.Tools.X509.verify_certificate.markdown:
```pike
"deprecated"object(TBSCertificate) verify_certificate(string s, mapping authorities)
```

Decodes a certificate, checks the signature. Returns the TBSCertificate structure, or 0 if decoding or verification failes.
Authorities is a mapping from (DER-encoded) names to a verifiers.
**Note:** This function allows self-signed certificates, and it doesn't check that names or extensions make sense.

predef.Tools.X509.verify_certificate_cha.signature: "deprecated"mapping verify_certificate_chain(array(string) cert_chain, mapping authorities, int|void require_trust)
predef.Tools.X509.verify_certificate_cha.markdown:
```pike
"deprecated"mapping verify_certificate_chain(array(string) cert_chain, mapping authorities, int|void require_trust)
```

Decodes a certificate chain, checks the signatures. Verifies that the chain is unbroken, and that all certificates are in effect (time-wise.)
Returns a mapping with the following contents, depending on the verification of the certificate chain:
**Mapping:**
  - "error_code": int — Error describing type of verification failure, if verification failed. May be one of the following: CERT_TOO_NEW, CERT_TOO_OLD, CERT_ROOT_UNTRUSTED, CERT_BAD_SIGNATURE, CERT_INVALID, CERT_UNAUTHORIZED_CA or CERT_CHAIN_BROKEN
  - "error_cert": int — Index number of the certificate that caused the verification failure.
  - "self_signed": int(0..1) — Non-zero if the certificate is self-signed.
  - "verified": int(0..1) — Non-zero if the certificate is verified.
  - "authority": string — Standards.ASN1.Sequence of the authority RDN that verified the chain.
  - "cn": string — Standards.ASN1.Sequence of the common name RDN of the leaf certificate.
- `cert_chain` — An array of certificates, with the relative-root last. Each certificate should be a DER-encoded certificate.
- `authorities` — A mapping from (DER-encoded) names to verifiers.
- `require_trust` — Require that the certificate be traced to an authority, even if it is self signed. See Standards.PKCS.Certificate.get_dn_string for converting the RDN to an X500 style string.

predef.Tools.Testsuite.run_script.markdown:
```pike
array(int) run_script(string|array(string) pike_script)
```

Runs an external pike script from a testsuite. Use Tools.Testsuite.report_result in the script to report how many tests succeeded, failed, and were skipped. Those numbers are returned in the array from this function.

predef.Tools.Testsuite.log_msg.markdown:
```pike
void log_msg(string msg, mixed ... args)
```

Logs a testsuite message to stderr. The message is shown regardless of the verbosity level. If the previous message was logged without a trailing newline then a newline is inserted first.
The message should normally have a trailing newline - no extra newline is added to it. Use log_status to log a message intended to be overwritten.

predef.Tools.Testsuite.log_msg_cont.markdown:
```pike
void log_msg_cont(string msg, mixed ... args)
```

Similar to log_msg, but doesn't insert a newline first if the previous message didn't end with one. Does however insert a newline if the previous message was logged "in place" by log_status.

predef.Tools.Testsuite.log_status.markdown:
```pike
void log_status(string msg, mixed ... args)
```

Logs a testsuite status message to stdout. This is suitable for nonimportant progress messages.
If the verbosity is 0 then nothing is logged, but the message is saved and will be logged if the next call is to log_msg. If the verbosity is 1, the message is "in place" on the current line without a trailing line feed, so that the next log_status message will replace this one. If the verbosity is 2 or greater, the message is logged with a trailing line feed.
The message should be short and not contain newlines, to work when the verbosity is 1, but if it ends with a newline then it's logged normally. It can be an empty string to just clear the last "in place" logged message - it won't be logged otherwise.
**See also:** log_twiddler

predef.Tools.Testsuite.log_twiddler.markdown:
```pike
void log_twiddler()
```

Logs a rotating bar for showing progress. Only output at the end of an "in place" message written by log_status on verbosity level 1.

predef.Tools.AutoDoc.ProcessXML.extractX.signature: string extractXML(string filename, int|void pikeMode, string|void type, string|void name, array(string)|void parentModules, void|object(.Flags) flags)
predef.Tools.AutoDoc.ProcessXML.extractX.markdown:
```pike
string extractXML(string filename, int|void pikeMode, string|void type, string|void name, array(string)|void parentModules, void|object(.Flags) flags)
```

This function extracts documentation from a file. The parameters type, name, and parentModules are used only when pikeMode != 0 and no C-style doc comments are present.
- `filename` — The file to extract from.
- `pikeMode` — Non-zero if it is a Pike file. If the file contains style doc comments, C-mode is used despite pikeMode != 0.
- `type` — `"class"`, `"module"` or `"namespace"`.
- `name` — The name of the class/module/namespace.
- `parentModules` — The ancestors of the class/module/namespace.
- `flags` — Flags adjusting the extractor behaviour. Defaults to FLAG_NORMAL.
**Example:** // To extract doc for Foo.Bar.Ippa: string xml = extractXML("lib/modules/Foo.pmod/Bar.pmod/Ippa.pike", 1, "class", "Ippa", ({ "Foo", "Bar" }));

predef.Tools.AutoDoc.ProcessXML.moveImag.signature: string moveImages(string docXMLFile, string imageSourceDir, string imageDestDir, int|void quiet)
predef.Tools.AutoDoc.ProcessXML.moveImag.markdown:
```pike
string moveImages(string docXMLFile, string imageSourceDir, string imageDestDir, int|void quiet)
```

Copy all images to canonical files in a flat directory.
- `docXMLFile` — The contents of the XML file. The XML file is the result of extraction from a single C or Pike file, for example the result of calling extractXML.
- `imageSourceDir` — The directory that is the base of the relative paths to images. This should be the directory of the source file that was the input to extract the XML file.
- `imageDestDir` — The directory where the images should be copied.
- `quiet` — Quiet operation.
**Returns:** The XML file contents (with decorated <image>-tags)

predef.Tools.AutoDoc.ProcessXML.mergeTre.markdown:
```pike
void mergeTrees(object(SimpleNode) dest, object(SimpleNode) source)
```

Puts all children of source into the tree dest, in their correct place module-hierarchically.
Used to merge the results of extractions of different Pike and C files.
Some minor effort is expended to normalize the result to some sort of canonical order.
- `source` — The nodes source and dest are `<class>`, `<module>`, `<namespace>` or `<autodoc>` nodes that are identical in the sense that they represent the same module, class or namespace. Typically they both represent `<autodoc>` nodes.
**Note:** Both source and dest are modified destructively.
**Note:** After calling this method, any `<class>` or `<module>` nodes that have been marked with @appears or @belongs, are still in the wrong place in the tree, so handleAppears() (or postProcess()) must be called on the whole documentation tree to relocate them once the tree has been fully merged.

predef.Tools.AutoDoc.ProcessXML.handleAp.markdown:
```pike
void handleAppears(object(SimpleNode) root, object(.Flags)|void flags)
```

Take care of all the @appears and @belongs directives everywhere, and rearranges the nodes in the tree accordingly
- `root` — The root (`<autodoc>`) node of the documentation tree.

predef.Tools.AutoDoc.ProcessXML.postProc.signature: void postProcess(object(SimpleNode) root, string|void logfile, object(.Flags)|void flags)
predef.Tools.AutoDoc.ProcessXML.postProc.markdown:
```pike
void postProcess(object(SimpleNode) root, string|void logfile, object(.Flags)|void flags)
```

Perform the last steps on a completed documentation tree.
- `root` — Root `<autodoc>` node of the completed documentation tree. Calls handleAppears(), cleanUndocumented() and resolveRefs() in turn.
**See also:** handleAppears(), cleanUndocumented(), resolveRefs()

predef.Thread.Fifo.markdown:
Fifo implements a fixed length first-in, first-out queue. A fifo is a queue of values and is often used as a stream of data between two threads.
**See also:** Queue

predef.Thread.Fifo.size.markdown:
```pike
int size()
```

This function returns the number of elements currently in the fifo.
**See also:** read(), write()

predef.Thread.Fifo.read.markdown:
```pike
mixed read()
```

This function retrieves a value from the fifo. Values will be returned in the order they were written. If there are no values present in the fifo the current thread will sleep until some other thread writes one.
**See also:** try_read(), read_array(), write()

predef.Thread.Fifo.try_read.markdown:
```pike
mixed try_read()
```

This function retrieves a value from the fifo if there is any there. Values will be returned in the order they were written. If there are no values present in the fifo then UNDEFINED will be returned.
**See also:** read()

predef.Thread.Fifo.read_array.markdown:
```pike
array read_array()
```

This function returns all values in the fifo as an array. The values in the array will be in the order they were written. If there are no values present in the fifo the current thread will sleep until some other thread writes one.
**See also:** read(), try_read_array()

predef.Thread.Fifo.try_read_array.markdown:
```pike
array try_read_array()
```

This function returns all values in the fifo as an array but doesn't wait if there are no values there. The values in the array will be in the order they were written.
**See also:** read_array()

predef.Thread.Fifo.write.markdown:
```pike
int write(mixed value)
```

Append a value to the end of the fifo. If there is no more room in the fifo the current thread will sleep until space is available. The number of items in the queue after the write is returned.
**See also:** read()

predef.Thread.Fifo.try_write.markdown:
```pike
int try_write(mixed value)
```

Append a value to the end of the fifo. If there is no more room in the fifo then zero will be returned, otherwise the number of items in the fifo after the write is returned.
**See also:** read()

predef.Thread.Fifo.create.markdown:
```pike
void create()
void create(int size)
```

Create a fifo. If the optional size argument is present it sets how many values can be written to the fifo without blocking. The default size is 128.

predef.Thread.Queue.markdown:
Queue implements a queue, or a pipeline. The main difference between Queue and Fifo is that Queue will never block in write(), only allocate more memory.
Ought to be made API-compatible with ADT.Queue.
**See also:** Fifo, ADT.Queue

predef.Thread.Queue.size.markdown:
```pike
int size()
```

This function returns the number of elements currently in the fifo.
**See also:** read(), write()

predef.Thread.Queue.read.markdown:
```pike
mixed read()
```

This function retrieves a value from the fifo. Values will be returned in the order they were written. If there are no values present in the fifo the current thread will sleep until some other thread writes one.
**See also:** try_read(), read_array(), write()

predef.Thread.Queue.try_read.markdown:
```pike
mixed try_read()
```

This function retrieves a value from the fifo if there is any there. Values will be returned in the order they were written. If there are no values present in the fifo then UNDEFINED will be returned.
**See also:** read()

predef.Thread.Queue.read_array.markdown:
```pike
array read_array()
```

This function returns all values in the fifo as an array. The values in the array will be in the order they were written. If there are no values present in the fifo the current thread will sleep until some other thread writes one.
**See also:** read(), try_read_array()

predef.Thread.Queue.try_read_array.markdown:
```pike
array try_read_array()
```

This function returns all values in the fifo as an array but doesn't wait if there are no values there. The values in the array will be in the order they were written.
**See also:** read_array()

predef.Thread.Queue.peek_array.markdown:
```pike
array peek_array()
```

Returns a snapshot of all the values in the queue, in the order they were written. The values are still left in the queue, so if other threads are reading from it, the returned value should be considered stale already on return.

predef.Thread.Queue.write.markdown:
```pike
int write(mixed value)
```

Append a value to the end of the fifo. If there is no more room in the fifo the current thread will sleep until space is available. The number of items in the queue after the write is returned.
**See also:** read()



**Returns:** Returns the result if available, a backtrace on failure, and `0` (zero) otherwise.


Register a callback to be called when the result is available.
- `to` — Callback to be called. The first argument to the callback will be the result or the failure backtrace, and the second `0` (zero) on success, and `1` on failure.



- `what` — The result to register.

predef.Thread.Farm.Handler.debug_status.markdown:
```pike
string debug_status()
```

Get some statistics about the worker thread.

predef.Thread.Farm.run_multiple.markdown:
```pike
object(Result) run_multiple(array(array(function|array)) fun_args)
```

Register multiple jobs.
- `fun_args` — An array of arrays where the first element is a function to call, and the second is a corresponding array of arguments.
**Returns:** Returns a Result object with an array with one element for the result for each of the functions in fun_args.
**Note:** Do not modify the elements of fun_args before the result is available.
**Note:** If any of the functions in fun_args throws and error, all of the accumulated results (if any) will be dropped from the result, and the first backtrace be provided.
**See also:** run_multiple_async()

predef.Thread.Farm.run_multiple_async.markdown:
```pike
void run_multiple_async(array fun_args)
```

Register multiple jobs where the return values are to be ignored.
- `fun_args` — An array of arrays where the first element is a function to call, and the second is a corresponding array of arguments.
**Note:** Do not modify the elements of fun_args before the result is available.
**See also:** run_multiple()

predef.Thread.Farm.run.markdown:
```pike
object(Result) run(function f, mixed ... args)
```

Register a job for the thread farm.
- `f` — Function to call with @args to perform the job.
- `args` — The parameters for f.
**Returns:** Returns a Result object for the job.
**Note:** In Pike 7.8 and earlier this function was broken and returned a Result object that wasn't connected to the job.
**See also:** run_async()

predef.Thread.Farm.run_async.markdown:


Provide a callback function to track names of threads created by the farm.
- `cb` — The callback function. This will get invoked with the thread as the first parameter and the name as the second whenever a thread is created. When the same thread terminates the callback is invoked again with 0 as the second parameter. Set cb to 0 to stop any previously registered callbacks from being called.
- `prefix` — An optional name prefix to distinguish different farms. If not given a prefix will be generated automatically.

predef.Yabu.DB.create.markdown:
```pike
void create(string dir, string mode)
```

Open a new or existing databse.
The dir is the directory the database should be stored in. It will be created if it does not exist.
Only one database can be in any given directory.
The mode specifies the operation mode, and is a string made up of the desired modes, 'r'=read, 'w'=write and 'c'=create.
To open an existing database in read only mode, use "r".
To open an existing database in read/write mode, use "rw".
To create a new database, or open an existing one in read write mode, use "rwc".

predef.Yabu.LookupDB.markdown:
This database is optimized for lots of very small data records (a few bytes each, usually), but the API is otherwise identical to the normal DB API.
It will perform badly if used with big records. You also need to know in advance aproximately how many keys there will be, the performance will degrade if way more than the expected number of keys are present.

predef.Yabu.LookupDB.create.markdown:
```pike
void create(string dir, string mode)
```

Open a new or existing databse.
The dir is the directory the database should be stored in. It will be created if it does not exist.
Only one database can be in any given directory.
The mode specifies the operation mode, and is a string made up of the desired modes, 'r'=read, 'w'=write and 'c'=create.
To open an existing database in read only mode, use "r".
To open an existing database in read/write mode, use "rw".
To create a new database, or open an existing one in read write mode, use "rwc".
